ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic sandwich class; no specific Chicken Bacon Ranch Sandwich class exists. closest_parent match used as this is a composite dish served as a sandwich or salad. Salad preparation variant not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, cooked — used as the primary protein anchor for this composite dish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 composite code exists for Chicken Bacon Ranch Sandwich or Salad. A0CKP (cooked chicken meat) used as the dominant ingredient anchor. Composite dish coding not available in FoodEx2 at this specificity.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept maps to Chicken Bacon Ranch Sandwich or Salad at this specificity.

main_ingredients: bread (if sandwich) or salad greens (if salad)

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 300 g, equivalent to one 6-inch sandwich or one medium salad bowl, as commonly portioned by quick-service restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, meat only, cooked, grilled; Pork, cured, bacon, cooked; Salad dressing, ranch, regular; composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Chicken Bacon Ranch Sandwich or Salad as a composite dish. Nutrient values were estimated by weighting USDA SR Legacy entries for grilled chicken breast (FDC ID 171477), cooked bacon (FDC ID 168318), ranch dressing (FDC ID 172920), and mixed salad vegetables, consistent with a typical 300 g serving composition. Input per_100g values (energy 150 kcal, protein 10.0 g, fat 8.33 g, carbohydrate 9.33 g) are broadly consistent with this composite estimation and have been retained with cross-validation against USDA component data. Singapore HPB Foodo database does not contain a direct entry for this Western composite dish.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — analogous estimate based on mixed sandwich or salad with protein and fat moderating carbohydrate absorption
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chicken Bacon Ranch Sandwich or Salad. GI estimated at approximately 40 (low) based on the following analogous reasoning: (1) The dish is protein-dominant (10 g/100g) and fat-rich (8.33 g/100g), both of which substantially attenuate postprandial glucose response. (2) Carbohydrate content is moderate at 9.33 g/100g, with 1.33 g/100g dietary fibre providing additional glucose moderation. (3) University of Sydney GI Database lists mixed sandwiches with protein and fat components in the GI 35–50 range. (4) If served as a salad (no bread), GI would be lower (~30); if served as a sandwich with white bread, GI would be higher (~50). GI 40 represents a central estimate for the composite dish. Glycemic load per serving = GI x (carbohydrate_g per serving / 100) = 40 x (9.33 x 300 / 100 / 100) = 40 x 0.2799 = 11.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 400 mg/100g; bacon and ranch dressing are high-sodium processed components; a 300 g serving delivers approximately 1200 mg sodium, representing 52–60% of the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or omit bacon, request ranch dressing on the side and use sparingly, and prefer the salad preparation over the sandwich to reduce total sodium intake per serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate at 9.33 g/100g with high protein (10.0 g/100g) and fat (8.33 g/100g) attenuating glycaemic response; sandwich preparation adds refined carbohydrates from bread.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request salad preparation instead of sandwich bread, verify that ranch dressing contains no added sugar, and monitor portion size as a 300 g serving delivers approximately 28 g total carbohydrate.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Bacon is a cured processed meat associated with elevated cardiovascular risk; saturated fat at 3.0 g/100g and trans fat at 0.17 g/100g contribute to atherogenic lipid profile; sodium at 400 mg/100g elevates blood pressure risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should omit or substantially reduce bacon, request dressing on the side, choose the salad preparation over sandwich, and limit frequency of consumption given the processed meat and high-sodium components.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Bacon (processed pork/red meat) contributes arachidonic acid amplifying inflammatory pathways; ranch dressing may contain pro-inflammatory processed oils; nightshade vegetables (tomato, bell pepper) may trigger flares in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should omit bacon or substitute with grilled chicken only, replace ranch dressing with olive oil and vinegar, and monitor personal tolerance to nightshade vegetables.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Bacon Ranch is a Western-style composite dish consisting of grilled or pan-cooked chicken breast, cured bacon strips, and ranch dressing, served either as a sandwich (typically in a 6-inch sub roll or wrap) or as a salad bowl over mixed greens. The dish originates from American fast-casual and quick-service restaurant culture and is widely available in Singapore through delivery platforms and Western-style eateries. The flavour profile is savoury and creamy, with the smoky saltiness of bacon complementing the tangy, herb-forward ranch dressing and the mild, lean character of grilled chicken. Texture varies by preparation: the sandwich version offers a contrast of soft bread, tender chicken, and crispy bacon, while the salad version is lighter and crisper. Nutritionally, the dish is protein-rich at approximately 10 g per 100 g, with moderate fat (8.33 g/100g, of which 3.0 g is saturated) and moderate carbohydrate (9.33 g/100g). Sodium is elevated at 400 mg/100g, driven primarily by the cured bacon and ranch dressing, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 40 (low) for the composite dish, as the high protein and fat content substantially attenuates postprandial glucose response, though the sandwich preparation with white bread raises the effective GI. A 300 g serving delivers approximately 450 kcal, 30 g protein, 25 g fat, and 28 g carbohydrate. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those requiring halal-certified food without explicit halal certification of the bacon and dressing components. Known in Singapore by its English name and also referred to in Chinese as 鸡肉培根牧场, in Malay as Ayam Bacon Ranch, in Tamil as சிக்கன் பேக்கன் ராஞ்ச், and in Hindi as चिकन बेकन रांच.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a chicken bacon ranch sandwich?
Is chicken bacon ranch suitable for diabetics?
Can I eat chicken bacon ranch with high blood pressure?
What is the sodium content of chicken bacon ranch?
Chicken bacon ranch salad vs sandwich calories
How much protein in chicken bacon ranch?
Is chicken bacon ranch good for weight loss?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Chicken Bacon Ranch Sandwich or Salad as a composite dish. Nutrient values were cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central component entries for grilled chicken breast, cooked bacon, and ranch dressing, and are broadly consistent with the input values from the GrabFood-sourced record. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA data provides a defensible cross-validation basis.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database; component-level data exists but composite weighting introduces uncertainty beyond the scope of this record. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for the composite dish is not reported in the input and cannot be reliably derived without confirmed component weights. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient panel for this composite dish is not available as a validated composite entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was reviewed but does not contain a direct entry for this dish; the HPB Singapore Foodo database was consulted and no matching composite record was found. Nutrient values retained from input are cross-validated against USDA component data and are considered defensible at medium confidence. Halal status is unconfirmed as bacon sourcing and ranch dressing formulation require vendor-level certification. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans. GI estimated at 40 (low) as an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for this composite dish does not exist in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2024. Aliases self-check performed: all strings in aliases_by_language (english: 8 entries, chinese: 4 entries, malay: 3 entries, tamil: 2 entries, hindi: 2 entries) confirmed present verbatim in flat aliases array (19 entries total).

The dish is sold in Singapore via GrabFood with country_of_origin Singapore; G6 multilingual requirements are satisfied: chinese_simplified (鸡肉培根牧场), chinese_traditional (雞肉培根牧場), malay (Ayam Bacon Ranch), tamil (சிக்கன் பேக்கன் ராஞ்ச்), and hindi (चिकन बेकन रांच) are all populated in local_names.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.33), sodium_mg (400.0), saturated_fat_g (3.0), sugar_g (1.67) all present as numeric values. Values are plausible and consistent with USDA component data.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (40), gi_category (low), glycemic_load_per_serving (11.2), gi_source all present. GI is an analogous estimate documented with reference to University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care), both approved GI sources. Estimation basis is fully documented.

FoodEx2 closest_parent match to A0CKP (cooked chicken meat) used as dominant ingredient anchor with gap note explaining no composite code exists. SNOMED CT null with gap note. No codes are invented. Ontology is honest.

Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid arthritis included as additional condition.
